short_extract_or_summary: Century separates technical/rules of art, skillful contrivance, human skill opposed to nature, imitation/substitute, feigned/not genuine, affected, and artful/crafty.
limitation: Online reproduction rather than original scan.

source_name: Webster 1913 / GNU Collaborative International Dictionary via Wordnik
short_extract_or_summary: Gives made/contrived by art or human skill opposed to natural, not genuine, obsolete artful/cunning, and technical rhetoric/science senses.
Defines artificially as by art or skill and contrivance, not by nature; also skillfully and craftily as obsolete senses.
limitation: Search result/aggregator; use only as support.

short_extract_or_summary: Gives artificial as made by art, not natural, cultivated, feigned, and affected in manners.
Secondary transcription: artifice includes trick/fraud/stratagem and art/trade.
Secondary Johnson transcription defines factitious as made by art, opposed to what is made by nature.
limitation: Comparator only; not a central artificial source.

short_extract_or_summary: Artifact is described as anything made by art or an artificial product.
